sub: Two people who just met. Small talk → real.

1.name: Perception
q: What made you say yes to tonight?
What's your honest first impression of me?
Guess one thing about me — I'll tell you if you're right.
What's something you're weirdly good at that never comes up?
Were you the same kid you are now, or a totally different person?
What's the last thing that genuinely made you laugh out loud?
If we skipped the small talk, what should I actually ask you?
What's a small thing that instantly makes you like someone?
What's your most-used app, and what does it say about you?
Are you a text-back-in-two-seconds person or a three-days-later person?
What could you talk about for an hour without getting bored?
What did you want to be when you were ten?
What's the best compliment you've ever been given?
Where do you feel most like yourself?
What's a tiny thing that ruins your day embarrassingly fast?
Mountains or the sea — and don't overthink it.
What's the story you always end up telling at dinners?
What do people assume about you that's just wrong?
What's your relationship with mornings?
Three words your friends would use for you — and would you agree?
What song would you put on to set the mood for a night like this?
What's the last thing that made you go 'I have to tell someone'?
What are you a little too competitive about?
What's your comfort order at 1am?
What's the most spontaneous thing you've done lately?
Do first impressions stick for you, or do people surprise you?
What's something you've changed your mind about recently?
What's a red flag you find weirdly attractive?
Describe your perfect Sunday.
What are you quietly hoping I'm like?
What's the last thing you Googled before you left the house tonight?
If your dating profile was one honest sentence, what would it say?
What's your go-to opening line, and does it actually work?
Guess my star sign — then tell me if you even believe in that stuff.
What's the most 'you' thing in your bag or pockets right now?
Cats or dogs, and what does that say about you?
What's a hill you'll die on that's completely ridiculous?
What would your group chat call this date if they were watching live?
What's your worst first-date story — a different one, not this one?
What show will you defend even though you know it's bad?
What's your tell when you're actually into someone?
If I scrolled your camera roll right now, what would embarrass you?
What trend were you way too late to catch onto?
What's your order when you're trying to impress someone?
What's the most impulsive thing sitting in your online cart right now?
What's your 'ick' — the small thing that kills it instantly?
Sunrise or sunset person, and when did you last actually catch one?
What's a compliment you'd give yourself that no one's said yet?
What's the emoji you use way too often?
What's a rule you break on purpose and don't feel bad about?
What would your villain origin story be?
What's the last thing you did that would shock your younger self?
If we swapped playlists right now, which song would give you away first?
What's your toxic trait — the one you're self-aware enough to actually admit?
What's a purchase you've made that's extremely 'you'?
What's on your bucket list that you haven't told many people about?
What's your walk-out song, if you had one?
Beach holiday or city break — pick fast, no hedging.
What's a hot take you hold that would start a fight at dinner?
What's the most 'main character energy' thing you've ever done?
Guess what I do for a living — closest guess gets the real answer.
What's your honest verdict on splitting the bill tonight?
Do you have a 'type,' and how close am I to it?
What's the last white lie you told, and was it a good one?
What outfit almost didn't make the cut before you left the house tonight?
Are you the friend who plans the whole trip, or the one who shows up with a bag five minutes before?
What's a word or phrase you say way too often — think you can go the rest of the night without it?
What's a skill you wish you had that has nothing to do with your job?
What conspiracy theory do you secretly enjoy, even if you don't believe a word of it?
What's the most chaotic thing happening in one of your group chats right now?
What song are you embarrassed to admit you know every word to?
What's your phone battery at right now — panicking yet?

2.name: Connection
q: What's a risk you're glad you didn't talk yourself out of?
Who had the biggest hand in making you who you are?
What are you working on about yourself right now?
When do you feel most alive?
What's a moment that quietly changed the direction of your life?
What did you have to unlearn from how you grew up?
What's something you want that you don't say out loud very often?
What are you more afraid of — being alone, or settling?
What does a good life look like to you — not the highlight-reel version?
When did you last cry, and did it help?
What version of yourself are you glad you outgrew?
What do you need more of right now?
What's the hardest thing you've been through that you're okay telling me?
What are you proud of that rarely comes up?
Who do you miss?
What's a belief you hold that most people around you don't?
When do you feel lonely, even around people?
What's the kindest thing someone has done for you?
What are you still trying to prove, and to whom?
What's something you've forgiven yourself for — or are trying to?
From what you've seen, what does real love actually look like?
What part of yourself do you protect the most?
What's a dream you've quietly put on a shelf?
What do you know you do when you're falling for someone?
What's the gap between who you are and who people think you are?
What fear has been running quietly in the background lately?
When did you last feel truly understood?
What would you do if you knew you couldn't fail — and what's stopping you now?
What do you wish someone would just know without you explaining?
What's something you have never once regretted saying yes to?
What's a pattern in your family you're consciously trying not to repeat?
What does 'home' mean to you these days?
When did you last surprise yourself?
What do you need from someone before you can actually feel safe with them?
Is there an apology you never got but still needed?
What's a value you'd never compromise on, even for the right person?
What did success look like to you ten years ago — and what does it look like now?
What's your love language, honestly, not the quiz version?
What are you fiercely loyal to?
Who believed in you before you believed in yourself?
Does ambition drive you or exhaust you, if you're honest?
What's something you've noticed you inherited from a parent, good or bad?
When did you actually become an adult — not your 18th birthday, the real moment?
What scares you about getting older?
What do you do when no one's watching that says the most about you?
What's your honest relationship with money, underneath the practical stuff?
What does 'enough' feel like to you?
What's something you want that you're almost scared to admit you want?
When did you last choose yourself over what someone expected of you?
Who would you call at 3am, no explanation needed?
Who or what have you lost that still shapes how you love people?
What's a pattern you keep repeating in relationships, even when you see it coming?
What do you need to hear more often that you never ask for?
When did you last feel like the outsider in a room?
Does failure wreck you or teach you?
What's a story you used to tell about yourself that isn't true anymore?
What's still healing, even if most of it already has?
What makes you feel chosen?
How honest can you actually be about needing people?
What are you quietly building toward right now?
What's something you believed as a kid that you're a little sad isn't true?
What does your inner critic sound like — whose voice is it, if you're honest?
What's a boundary you had to learn to hold, and who taught you the hard way?
When have you been braver for someone else than you've ever been for yourself?
What's a fear you've actually made peace with?
What's a risk you talked yourself out of that you still think about?
What does grief look like for you — fast and loud, or slow and quiet?
How do you take care of yourself on the days that are quietly hard?
What's a piece of advice you give often but still struggle to take yourself?
What's something you're grateful for now that you resented at the time?
What's something you're quietly afraid people would think less of you for, if they knew?

3.name: Reflection
q: What have you noticed about me tonight that surprised you?
What's something you almost said earlier but held back?
How do you feel right now, in this exact moment?
What's one thing you already like about me?
When did you feel closest to me tonight?
What are you curious about that you haven't asked me yet?
What do you think I'm like when no one's watching?
Has anything about me shifted since your first impression?
What would you regret not telling me before tonight ends?
What's a question you're hoping I'll ask you?
What do you think we have in common that isn't obvious?
Do you feel like yourself around me right now?
What about tonight will you still remember next week?
What's the bravest thing you could say to me right now?
If tonight is all we get, what do you want me to remember about you?
What are you protecting yourself from with me?
What do you hope happens after tonight?
What's something you want to thank me for?
If you're honest, where do you feel this going?
What do you want more of, from me, right now?
What's true for you that's hard to say out loud?
What did you need to hear tonight?
How did I make you feel tonight?
What's the realest thing you can tell me before we call it?
What's one thing about this you don't want to forget?
What do you want to happen in the next five minutes?
Gut reaction — yes or no to a second date? Don't think about it.
What are you nervous to admit you're already feeling?
What's a physical detail about me you've noticed and haven't said out loud?
If tonight were a movie, what would you cut from the script?
What version of tonight are you going to tell your friends tomorrow?
What does the silence between us feel like right now?
Do you trust your gut about this? What's it telling you?
What do you want me to ask you that I haven't yet?
Is anything making you hesitate right now? Say it.
What would you say if you were braver than you're being right now?
Does this feel like a beginning or a nice one-off — and which are you hoping for?
What would you change about how tonight started, if anything?
What's a promise you'd make if this turns into something?
What did tonight teach you about what you actually want?
What's the question you're hoping I don't ask you?
What does chemistry feel like to you, physically, right now?
Do you believe in knowing early? Is this that?
What would you do if I told you right now that I like you?
What's a line you won't cross tonight, and why does it matter to you?
If you said yes to me tonight, what would you want it to mean?
Have you thought about kissing me tonight? Be honest.
What are you proud of yourself for, in how you showed up tonight?
What's the bravest question you could ask me right now?
What would you regret not doing before we say goodnight?
If I had to guess your favorite moment from tonight, what would I pick — and would I be right?
What's changed in how you're sitting or holding yourself since we first sat down?
What's a question I asked tonight that you answered less than completely honestly?
If I leaned in right now, what would you do?
What's something you hope I don't compare you to — an ex, a type, anything?
Is there a version of you I haven't met yet — and when do I get to?
What's the answer you're most afraid I'll give you tonight?
On a scale of nervous to completely comfortable, where are you right now — and has that number moved tonight?
What does my laugh do to you, if anything?
What do you want to be doing with your hands right now that you're not?
What's the last thing I said that's still sitting with you?

wild: Hold eye contact for ten seconds. No talking. Then say the first word that comes.
Show me the last photo you took — no cheating, no explaining first.
Trade a secret. Small is fine. You go first.
Describe me to a stranger in one sentence.
Play the song that matches this exact moment.
Give me a genuine compliment — the kind you'd usually keep to yourself.
Read me your last text and tell me the story behind it.
Guess my love language. Then tell me yours.
Swap phones and pick the other's next song. No previews.
Sit in silence together for fifteen seconds. Notice how it feels.
Tell me one thing you like about me, starting with 'I noticed…'
Say something you'd only say a few drinks from now.
Rate tonight out of ten — and say what would make it a ten.
Tell me the thought you almost didn't share.
Take a photo of me right now, exactly as I look. Keep it.
Finish this out loud: 'What I'm not saying is…'
Do an impression of the other person's laugh.
Show them your camera roll's most-used folder or album — no explaining first.
Text one friend right now: 'rate this date so far.' Read the reply out loud.
Order for each other off the menu — no asking, just decide.
Show the last thing you searched on your phone. No skipping.
Give each other a nickname right now. It has to stick for the rest of the night.
Swap seats and answer the next card as if you were them.
Write a one-line review of this date so far. Read it out loud.
Raise a toast to how you ended up here tonight.
Show the name of the group chat you talk in most.
Draw each other from memory in under thirty seconds. No peeking.
Hand over your phone. They read your last three notifications, no explaining.
Slow dance for ten seconds. No music needed.
Say the nickname your friends would give this date if they were watching.
Take one selfie together. No retakes, no filters.
Whisper your answer to the next card instead of saying it out loud.
Trade phones for one minute and change each other's contact name to something honest.
Set a one-minute timer and take turns naming everything you find attractive about the other person — no repeats, no stopping early.
Swap a piece of jewelry or an accessory and wear it for the rest of the night.
Act out how you'd introduce each other to your best friend, right now, in character.
final: Before you leave each other tonight, open your notes.
Write down one thing you want them to know — the thing you'd regret not saying.
Don't read it out. Send it once you're home.
